1.1: Paul (humble), a prisoner of Christ (the Anointed One) Jesus (YHWH saves), and Timothy (honoring God) our brother, To Philemon (affectionate) our beloved {agap?t?} fellow worker,
1.2: to Apphia (increasing) our sister, to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church {ekkl?sia} that meets at your house:
1.3: Grace and peace to you? from God {Theou} our Father and the Lord {Kyriou} Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One).
1.4: I always thank my God {The?}, remembering you in my prayers,
1.5: because I hear about your faith in the Lord {Kyrion} Jesus (YHWH saves) and your love {agap?n} for all the saints (holy ones).
1.6: I pray that your partnership {koin?nia} in the faith may become effective as you fully acknowledge every good thing that is ours in Christ (the Anointed One).
1.7: I take great joy and encouragement in your love {agap?}, because you, brother, have refreshed the hearts of the saints (holy ones).
1.8: So although in Christ (the Anointed One) I am bold enough to order you to do what is proper,
1.9: I prefer to appeal on the basis of love {agap?n}. For I, Paul (humble), am now aged, and a prisoner of Christ (the Anointed One) Jesus (YHWH saves) as well.
1.10: I appeal to you for my child Onesimus (useful), whose father I became while I was in chains.
1.11: Formerly he was useless to you, but now he has become useful both to you and to me.
1.12: I am sending back to you him who is my very heart.
1.13: I would have liked to keep him with me, so that on your behalf he could minister to me in my chains for the gospel (good news).
1.14: But I did not want to do anything without your consent, so that your goodness will not be out of compulsion, but by your own free will.
1.15: For perhaps this is why he was separated from you for a while, so that you might have him back for good—
1.16: no longer as a slave {doulon}, but better than a slave, as a beloved {agap?ton} brother. He is especially beloved to me, but even more so to you, both in person and in the Lord {Kyri?}.
1.17: So if you consider me a partner, receive him as you would receive me.
1.18: But if he has wronged you in any way or owes you anything, charge it to my account.
1.19: I, Paul (humble), write this with my own hand. I will repay it—not to mention that you owe me your very self.
1.20: Yes, brother, let me have some benefit {onaim?n} from you in the Lord {Kyri?}. Refresh my heart in Christ (the Anointed One).
1.21: Confident of your obedience, I write to you, knowing that you will do even more than I ask.
1.22: In the meantime, prepare a guest room for me, because I hope that through your? prayers I will be restored to you?.
1.23: Epaphras, my fellow prisoner in Christ (the Anointed One) Jesus (YHWH saves), sends you greetings,
1.24: as do Mark, Aristarchus (of Macedonia), Demas, and Luke (from Lucania), my fellow workers.
1.25: The grace of the Lord {Kyriou} Jesus (YHWH saves) Christ (the Anointed One) be with your? spirit [Amen.].
